title: alchemilla vulgaris var. curtiloba
intro: Alchemilla vulgaris var. curtiloba, also known as the lady's mantle, is a perennial flowering plant in the rose family that is native to Europe and Asia. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The medicinal properties of Alchemilla vulgaris var. curtiloba are believed to provide a range of health benefits, including soothing digestive issues, reducing menstrual cramps, and alleviating symptoms of menopause. The plant is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or dried extract, and is used in herbal preparations to support women's health and alleviate various other health concerns.
